speaker: TALBOT
text: Where is my strength, my valor, and my force?

speaker: TALBOT
text: Our English troops retire; I cannot stay them.

speaker: TALBOT
text: A woman clad in armor chaseth them.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Here, here she comes!—I’ll have a bout with thee.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Devil or devil’s dam, I’ll conjure thee.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Blood will I draw on thee—thou art a witch—

speaker: TALBOT
text: And straightway give thy soul to him thou serv’st.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Come, come; ’tis only I that must disgrace thee.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Heavens, can you suffer hell so to prevail?

speaker: TALBOT
text: My breast I’ll burst with straining of my courage,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And from my shoulders crack my arms asunder,

speaker: TALBOT
text: But I will chastise this high-minded strumpet.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Talbot, farewell. Thy hour is not yet come.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: I must go victual Orleance forthwith.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: O’ertake me if thou canst. I scorn thy strength.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Go, go, cheer up thy hunger-starvèd men.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Help Salisbury to make his testament.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: This day is ours, as many more shall be.

speaker: TALBOT
text: My thoughts are whirlèd like a potter’s wheel.

speaker: TALBOT
text: I know not where I am nor what I do.

speaker: TALBOT
text: A witch by fear—not force, like Hannibal—

speaker: TALBOT
text: Drives back our troops, and conquers as she lists.

speaker: TALBOT
text: So bees with smoke and doves with noisome stench

speaker: TALBOT
text: Are from their hives and houses driven away.

speaker: TALBOT
text: They called us, for our fierceness, English dogs;

speaker: TALBOT
text: Now like to whelps we crying run away.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Hark, countrymen, either renew the fight,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Or tear the lions out of England’s coat.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Renounce your soil; give sheep in lions’ stead.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Sheep run not half so treacherous from the wolf,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Or horse or oxen from the leopard,

speaker: TALBOT
text: As you fly from your oft-subduèd slaves.

speaker: TALBOT
text: It will not be! Retire into your trenches.

speaker: TALBOT
text: You all consented unto Salisbury’s death,

speaker: TALBOT
text: For none would strike a stroke in his revenge.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Pucelle is entered into Orleance

speaker: TALBOT
text: In spite of us or aught that we could do.

speaker: TALBOT
text: O, would I were to die with Salisbury!

speaker: TALBOT
text: The shame hereof will make me hide my head.
